structure_blocks.heading: Developing a procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: Developing a procurement strategy

structure_blocks.heading: Developing a procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: The development of the procurement strategy begins with initial discussions between you as the buyer and the client. It is the most important step in the procurement process, as it influences the scope of the requirement and determines the level of competition.

structure_blocks.heading: Developing a procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: You must consider the Public Services and Procurement Canada’s ( PSPC ) Guiding principles when developing a procurement strategy.

structure_blocks.heading: Meeting operational and legal requirements
structure_blocks.text: Meeting operational and legal requirements

structure_blocks.heading: Meeting operational and legal requirements
structure_blocks.text: Ensure the procurement strategy satisfies the client's operational requirements. Make sure it also complies with legal requirements, while achieving best value, and advancing national objectives.

structure_blocks.heading: Providing a challenge function
structure_blocks.text: To maintain the integrity of the procurement process, you should provide a challenge function during the procurement strategy stage. This function helps ensure transparency and fairness.

structure_blocks.heading: Providing a challenge function
structure_blocks.text: Examples of applying this function include:

structure_blocks.heading: Providing a challenge function
structure_blocks.text: advocating for early engagement with industry when possible

structure_blocks.heading: Providing a challenge function
structure_blocks.text: challenging any proposed non-competitive procurements to ensure the justification is well-supported and can withstand public scrutiny

structure_blocks.heading: Providing a challenge function
structure_blocks.text: ensuring specific requirements aren't overly restrictive, which could unnecessarily limit competition

structure_blocks.heading: Providing a challenge function
structure_blocks.text: confirming the appropriate solicitation method is used

structure_blocks.heading: Providing a challenge function
structure_blocks.text: using advanced contract award notices only when suitable

structure_blocks.heading: Providing a challenge function
structure_blocks.text: documenting all decisions related to dividing or unbundling a requirement into smaller parts, ensuring these decisions aren't intended to bypass financial approval thresholds or policy requirements, such as contract entry limits or trade agreement obligations

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: Considerations for the procurement strategy

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: In developing the most effective procurement strategy, you and the client must consider a few factors. If applicable, you must:

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: choose the most suitable method of supply for the procurement

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: assess the total estimated cost, including all options, maintenance, and storage costs, ensuring a comprehensive understanding of the financial implications of the entire lifecycle cost of the procurement

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: determine the contract period, including any options for extension

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: consider delivery requirements, including specific delivery timelines or milestones

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: plan the procurement schedule, detailing the procurement activities can help the project stay on time

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: establish evaluation procedures and a method of selection, detailing the criteria for evaluating offers and selecting the supplier

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: consider the environmental impact of procurement decisions

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: weigh the benefits of procuring commercial (off-the-shelf products) products against customized solutions

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: identify potential risks associated with the procurement and mitigation strategies

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: set clear terms for liability and indemnification

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: decide on the involvement of a fairness monitor to ensure the integrity of the procurement process

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: allow small and medium enterprises to participate

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: incorporate opportunities for Indigenous businesses

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: ensure the procurement meets accessibility standards

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: align the procurement with broader government national objectives

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: ensure new procurements are compatible with existing systems or solutions

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: look for opportunities to consolidate procurements for efficiency and cost savings

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: consider the end-of-life disposal of the product

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for the procurement strategy
structure_blocks.text: plan for the renewal or replacement of goods or services, including all transfer costs

structure_blocks.heading: Approval process
structure_blocks.text: Approval process

structure_blocks.heading: Approval process
structure_blocks.text: You must give the approval authority the opportunity to approve or reject the proposed procurement strategy as early as possible in the process. Preventing them from investing significant effort and time in a strategy that may not be approved. The procurement strategy must identify any deviations to contracting policies. Learn more about how to Prepare approval documents .

structure_blocks.heading: Approval process
structure_blocks.text: Note : To ensure alignment with original objectives and compliance with policy, you must seek re-approval for any major changes to the procurement strategy during the process.

raw_text:
Developing a procurement strategy

The development of the procurement strategy begins with initial discussions between you as the buyer and the client. It is the most important step in the procurement process, as it influences the scope of the requirement and determines the level of competition.

You must consider the Public Services and Procurement Canada’s ( PSPC ) Guiding principles when developing a procurement strategy.

Meeting operational and legal requirements

Ensure the procurement strategy satisfies the client's operational requirements. Make sure it also complies with legal requirements, while achieving best value, and advancing national objectives.

Conducting complete pricing and risk assessment

Assess pricing considerations, options and potential risks early on and throughout the acquisition lifecycle. Align the pricing strategy to the procurement strategy. Refer to sections 1, 2 and 3 of the Practitioner’s Guide for Procurement Pricing for more information.

Providing a challenge function

To maintain the integrity of the procurement process, you should provide a challenge function during the procurement strategy stage. This function helps ensure transparency and fairness.

Examples of applying this function include:

advocating for early engagement with industry when possible

challenging any proposed non-competitive procurements to ensure the justification is well-supported and can withstand public scrutiny

ensuring specific requirements aren't overly restrictive, which could unnecessarily limit competition

confirming the appropriate solicitation method is used

using advanced contract award notices only when suitable

documenting all decisions related to dividing or unbundling a requirement into smaller parts, ensuring these decisions aren't intended to bypass financial approval thresholds or policy requirements, such as contract entry limits or trade agreement obligations

Considerations for the procurement strategy

In developing the most effective procurement strategy, you and the client must consider a few factors. If applicable, you must:

choose the most suitable method of supply for the procurement

assess the total estimated cost, including all options, maintenance, and storage costs, ensuring a comprehensive understanding of the financial implications of the entire lifecycle cost of the procurement

determine the contract period, including any options for extension

consider delivery requirements, including specific delivery timelines or milestones

plan the procurement schedule, detailing the procurement activities can help the project stay on time

establish evaluation procedures and a method of selection, detailing the criteria for evaluating offers and selecting the supplier

consider the environmental impact of procurement decisions

weigh the benefits of procuring commercial (off-the-shelf products) products against customized solutions

identify potential risks associated with the procurement and mitigation strategies

set clear terms for liability and indemnification

decide on the involvement of a fairness monitor to ensure the integrity of the procurement process

allow small and medium enterprises to participate

incorporate opportunities for Indigenous businesses

ensure the procurement meets accessibility standards

align the procurement with broader government national objectives

ensure new procurements are compatible with existing systems or solutions

look for opportunities to consolidate procurements for efficiency and cost savings

consider the end-of-life disposal of the product

plan for the renewal or replacement of goods or services, including all transfer costs

Approval process

You must give the approval authority the opportunity to approve or reject the proposed procurement strategy as early as possible in the process. Preventing them from investing significant effort and time in a strategy that may not be approved. The procurement strategy must identify any deviations to contracting policies. Learn more about how to Prepare approval documents .

Note : To ensure alignment with original objectives and compliance with policy, you must seek re-approval for any major changes to the procurement strategy during the process.
